Layout.name: BlockTemplate

StyledText.Data: FAT Block template: <<Name>> Page items using this template: <<Number of page items>>

The name of the building block should be a descriptive name that gives a hint of the purpose of the block. Ex. “Chapter navigation button” or “Page headline”. The name will be JSON-ified in the reference. WARNING! This name should not be changed without informing the developers, since it is referenced in the code.

CharacterStyle.Text: WARNING! This name should not be changed without informing the developers, since it is referenced in the code.

StyledText.Data: The description of the building block can be viewed by the editor.

Calculation.Text: "Type a label name for this textfield"

StyledText.Data: This is the text on the layout where the editor should enter the text. Ex. “Name of job role”, “Plain text”. WARNING! Label names should not be changed without informing the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

CharacterStyle.Text: WARNING! Label names should not be changed without informing the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

Calculation.Text: "Type a label name for this textfield"
"Type a label name for this textfield"
"Type a label name for this textfield"
"Type a label name for this textfield"
"Type a label name for this textfield"
"Type a label name for this textfield"

StyledText.Data: This number defines the recommended maximum of characters for the input field to make the final presentation look good. Leave blank for no limit.

If this option is checked, a formatting bar will be visible above the text input field. The formatting bar is only visible in panels including “html” in their name. This option can be changed at any time during development.

If this option is checked, the text entered in the page item will be directly accessible in the page item JSON and not referenced via a text id in the text JSON file. The text in the JSON will be URL encoded. This can be useful when entering a URL or a value in the textfield. This option can be changed at any time during development.

Calculation.Text: BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <1 Or BlockTemplate::Text 1 Style type
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <1 Or BlockTemplate::Text 1 Style type ≠ "heading"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <1 Or BlockTemplate::Text 1 Style type ≠ "body"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <1 Or BlockTemplate::Text 1 Style type ≠ "other"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <2 Or BlockTemplate::Text 2 Style type
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <2 Or BlockTemplate::Text 2 Style type ≠ "heading"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <2 Or BlockTemplate::Text 2 Style type ≠ "body"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <2 Or BlockTemplate::Text 2 Style type ≠ "other"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <3 Or BlockTemplate::Text 3 Style type
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <3 Or BlockTemplate::Text 3 Style type ≠ "heading"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields < 3 Or BlockTemplate::Text 3 Style type ≠ "body"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <3 Or BlockTemplate::Text 3 Style type ≠ "other"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <4 Or BlockTemplate::Text 4 Style type
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <4 Or BlockTemplate::Text 4 Style type ≠ "heading"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <4 Or BlockTemplate::Text 4 Style type ≠ "body"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <4 Or BlockTemplate::Text 4 Style type ≠ "other"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <5 Or BlockTemplate::Text 5 Style type
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <5 Or BlockTemplate::Text 5 Style type ≠ "heading"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <5 Or BlockTemplate::Text 5 Style type ≠ "body"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <5 Or BlockTemplate::Text 5 Style type ≠ "other"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <6 Or BlockTemplate::Text 6 Style type
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <6 Or BlockTemplate::Text 6 Style type ≠ "heading"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <6 Or BlockTemplate::Text 6 Style type ≠ "body"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <6 Or BlockTemplate::Text 6 Style type ≠ "other"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <7 Or BlockTemplate::Text 7 Style type
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <7 Or BlockTemplate::Text 7 Style type ≠ "heading"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <7 Or BlockTemplate::Text 7 Style type ≠ "body"
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <7 Or BlockTemplate::Text 7 Style type ≠ "other"
"Type a label name for this mediafield"
"Type a label name for this mediafield"

StyledText.Data: If this option is checked, the media card window in FAT will enable settings for: - Crop - Fit It will also change the Media key in the Page item JSON to change from a String to a JSONObject.

If this option is checked, the media card window in FAT will enable settings for: - Selecting a Media IO Warning: It will also change the Media key in the Page item JSON from a String to a JSONObject.

Calculation.Text: "Type a name for the list header"
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 1

StyledText.Data: This is the visible text label on the layout where the editor should enter text or media. Ex. “Option”, “Feedback”. WARNING! Label names should not be changed without informing the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

CharacterStyle.Text: WARNING! Label names should not be changed without informing the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

Calculation.Text: List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 1
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 2
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 3
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 1
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 2
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 2
List template @ Block template::Number of value fields = 0

StyledText.Data: The Value is saved in the PageItem JSON and used for different purposes - if an alternative answer is correct, the sort order of a drag and drop activity and similar. Enter what kind of value type you want the user to enter - Boolean (dropdown menu, true/false), Number, String or None.

Calculation.Text: BlockTemplate::CA number of value fields OLD = 0
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 1
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 2
List template @ Block template::Number of value fields = 0
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 3

StyledText.Data: A content array is an array of items. Each item can contain textfields, media containers and value fields. The number of items can vary between different page items built with the same block. Content arrays are used when creating options for a question for example. Here are some typical content arrays: Radio button options in text 1 Text field. Label name “Option” 1 Value field, Type = Boolean. Label name “Correct option” (= true, otherwise false) Sort images 1 Media container. Label name “Image” 1 Value field, Type = Number. Label name “Sort order” Checkbox question with feedback 2 Text fields. Label name “Option” and “Feedback” 1 Value field, Type = Boolean. Label name “Correct option” (= true, otherwise false) Select how many of each field type you want your content array to contain.

Calculation.Text: List template @ Block template::Number of media containers = 0

StyledText.Data: A content array is an array of items. The min and max items numbers sets limits for the authoring popup menu when creating a page item from the symbol. Example: Settting Min. items = 3 and Max. items = 6. Author popup up menu contain the numbers 3, 4, 5 and 6.

Calculation.Text: List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 1
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 2
List template @ Block template::Number of text fields < 3
List template @ Block template::Number of media containers <2
BlockTemplate::Page item List enabled=1 Or BlockTemplate::List enabled = 1
BlockTemplate::Page item List enabled=1 Or BlockTemplate::List enabled = 1
"Select IO type if block uses array"
"Set primary IO type for this block"
"Type a description of how the IO is used here"

StyledText.Data: A short documentation what this block reads and write to the backend. This text will be visible for the author in Page item settings.

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "modifier_template_type" ; ModifierTemplate @ BlockTemplate::Type ) & SetProperty ( "modifier_template_id" ; ModifierTemplate @ BlockTemplate::PrimaryKey )

StyledText.Data: You can choose any name for your attribute. However, the name will be “prettified” using _underscore_in the JSON key.
The attribute description will be visible for authors and editors when adding content and entering key values to the FAT.

Choose attribute type depending on how you want to the JSON key value to be formatted. Prettified string Will replace capital letters with lower-case, åäö with aao and spaces with dash “-”. Number An integer value. If empty it will be replaced with 0 (zero). URL encoded string A string where all characters except A-Z, a-z are replaced with %. Media reference Will enable a media container as an attribute input. The value will be the prettified file name of the media file in the container. The media itself will be included in the media export. String Unaltered key value. Depending on characters, this might create a corrupt JSON. CSS Key values with type CSS will be exported to file “src/assets/css/cmt.scss”. The CSS key value can only be used for the Learning programme level. Value List Radio A list with pre-defined string values and a radio-button interface. Previously known as statetoggle.

CharacterStyle.Text: Key values with type CSS will be exported to file “src/assets/css/cmt.scss”. The CSS key value can only be used for the Learning programme level.
A list with pre-defined string values and a radio-button interface. Previously known as statetoggle.

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gModifierName ) Or IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gModifierType )

StyledText.Data: This is the default value when creating a new page item based on this building block. The default value must be a valid value.
Type a comma-separated list with the different values you want the user to select from. Example “blue,red,green,gray”
The attribute description will be visible for authors and editors when adding content and entering key values to the FAT.
Choose which is the lowest privilege set that will be able to edit this modifier value. Developer = 4 Author = 3 Editor = 2 Translator = 1
All global texts attached to this block template
Update all blocks using this template
View all titles using this block template

Calculation.Text: PatternCount(Self; "Sketched Coded Styled") Or PatternCount(Self; "Requires maintenance")

StyledText.Data: Is IO write-enabled or not? This flag helps FAT to create IOs automatically for fast authoring. The flag decides visiblity of a creation button in IO Page item setting interface.

Calculation.Text: Not PatternCount ( Baseproject Codebase @ BlockTemplate::Short name ; "version" )
Not PatternCount ( Baseproject Codebase @ BlockTemplate::Short name ; "version" )
Not PatternCount ( Baseproject Codebase @ BlockTemplate::Short name ; "version" )
Not PatternCount ( Baseproject Codebase @ BlockTemplate::Short name ; "version" )
"Page type if this block is a parent to other blocks."

StyledText.Data: Is this block Insight enabled or not? This flag helps FAT to decide what options are available for IO values on a page item. Insight enbled blocks does not set read and write IO context on the block.

Can this block use AI aspects when analysing free text input or not? This flag helps FAT to decide what options are available for IO values on a page item. AI aspects-enbled blocks makes it possible to select which aspects from the list that should be anaysed.

Does this Block have a sync between a specific IO field and a list, i.e. for categorising Posts? If true, FAT will guide the Author to connect the List to the right IO with a popup menu, showing IOs that have one or more IO Fields using the same list.
